attractions.name: Solang Valley
attractions.description: A mountain valley near Manali famous for paragliding, zorbing, and cable car rides in summer, and skiing lessons in winter snow.
attractions.transport_tips: 13 km from Manali, taxi or shared cab

attractions.name: Hidimba Devi Temple
attractions.description: A striking wooden pagoda-style temple set in a cedar forest, dedicated to Hidimba from the Mahabharata, built in 1553.
attractions.transport_tips: Short walk from Manali town centre

attractions.name: Old Manali
attractions.description: A laid-back village across the river from main Manali, known for cafes, backpacker guesthouses, and a bohemian atmosphere with mountain views.
attractions.transport_tips: 15-20 min walk from the Mall Road

attractions.name: Rohtang Pass
attractions.description: A high mountain pass (~3,978m) offering dramatic snow-capped views and the gateway to Lahaul-Spiti; entry is permit-restricted with a daily vehicle cap and it closes in heavy winter snow.
attractions.transport_tips: Requires an online permit booked in advance; shared taxis available from Manali

attractions.name: Vashisht Village Hot Springs
attractions.description: A village known for natural sulphur hot water springs and an ancient stone temple dedicated to sage Vashisht.
attractions.transport_tips: 3 km from Manali, short taxi ride

attractions.name: Atal Tunnel
attractions.description: One of the world's longest high-altitude tunnels, connecting the Manali valley to Lahaul, cutting travel time and offering a dramatic drive through the mountain.
attractions.transport_tips: Combine with a Sissu waterfall visit on the other side

attractions.name: Naggar Castle
attractions.description: A 15th-century mud-and-timber castle turned heritage hotel and museum, near the Nicholas Roerich Art Gallery, with valley views.

attractions.name: Jogini Waterfall Trek
attractions.description: A moderate 2-3 km trek from Vashisht village through pine forest to a scenic multi-tier waterfall.

restaurants.name: Johnson's Cafe, Manali
restaurants.description: A long-standing favourite for trout fish dishes, wood-fired pizza, and a garden seating area.

restaurants.name: Cafe 1947, Old Manali
restaurants.description: A riverside cafe popular for its Israeli and continental menu and relaxed mountain-view seating.

restaurants.name: Chopsticks Restaurant
restaurants.description: A Mall Road institution for Tibetan and Chinese momos, thukpa, and noodle dishes.

hidden_gems.name: Gulaba Snow Point
hidden_gems.description: A quieter, closer alternative to Rohtang Pass for snow views without the permit hassle, especially in early winter.

hidden_gems.name: Van Vihar National Park
hidden_gems.description: A quiet deodar-forest park with a small lake and boating, right by the Mall Road but far less crowded than the main strip.

nightlife.name: Old Manali Cafe Strip
nightlife.description: Old Manali's riverside cafes double as low-key evening hangouts with live acoustic music in season — Manali's nightlife is cafe-and-bonfire style rather than club-based.

shopping.name: Manali Mall Road
shopping.description: The main shopping street for Kullu shawls, Himachali caps, woollens, and local handicrafts, alongside restaurants and bakeries.

shopping.name: Tibetan Market
shopping.description: A market near the bus stand selling woollen jackets, boots, and winter accessories, popular for affordable winter wear.

festivals.name: Manali Winter Carnival
festivals.description: A winter festival with skiing competitions, cultural performances, and a carnival atmosphere across the valley.

adventure_activities.name: Paragliding at Solang Valley
adventure_activities.description: Tandem paragliding flights over the Solang valley with certified instructors, weather permitting.

adventure_activities.name: River Rafting on the Beas
adventure_activities.description: White-water rafting stretches on the Beas river near Kullu, roughly an hour from Manali.

local_tips: Rohtang Pass requires an online permit with a daily vehicle quota — book at least a few days ahead in peak season.
Pack layered warm clothing even in summer; mountain weather changes quickly and evenings are cold year-round.
Old Manali and main Manali are separated by the Manalsu river — both are walkable but taxis are cheap for the uphill return.
Mobile network connectivity can be patchy beyond Solang Valley and towards Rohtang.

safety.general: Manali is generally safe for tourists, but altitude-related routes (Rohtang, Solang in winter) carry real weather and road risk — check conditions before travelling.
safety.altitude: Some visitors feel mild altitude effects above Rohtang; stay hydrated and avoid overexertion on arrival.
safety.road_safety: Mountain roads have sharp hairpins and landslide risk in monsoon (Jul-Sep) — avoid non-essential travel during heavy rain.

culture.overview: Manali sits in the Kullu valley, with a strong Himachali mountain culture, wooden temple architecture, and a large seasonal backpacker/traveller community in Old Manali.
culture.etiquette: Dress modestly at temples; ask before photographing local people, especially in smaller villages.

transport.getting_around: Taxis and shared cabs cover most sightseeing; Old Manali and the Mall Road area are walkable. Bike/scooter rentals are available for more independent exploring.
transport.access: Nearest airport is Bhuntar (Kullu-Manali Airport), ~50 km away; most travellers arrive by overnight Volvo bus or taxi from Delhi/Chandigarh.

visa_information.domestic_indian_travellers: No visa needed — valid photo ID required for hotel check-in and for the Rohtang Pass permit.
visa_information.foreign_nationals: Standard Indian visa requirements apply; carry passport copies for permit applications.

weather.best_time_to_visit: March-June (pleasant summer) and Dec-Feb (snow), depending on whether you want snow or greenery
weather.avoid: Heavy monsoon weeks (Jul-Aug) for landslide risk on mountain roads
